page.title: Is October a Good Month for Yacht Charter in Marbella?

data.meta_description: Is October a good month for yacht charter in Marbella? Discover October weather, sea temps, pricing, and top boats for a late-season Mediterranean escape.
data.summary: October offers warm days, calm seas, and lower rates for a Marbella yacht charter. This guide covers weather averages, sea temperatures, and which boats are best for late-season cruising.
key_facts: Rainfall: 4–6 rainy days on average (40–60 mm total)
Puerto Banús departures: all boats available on request
Fewer crowds at Marbella Marina and Cabopino beaches

faq.q: Is it warm enough to swim in Marbella in October?
faq.a: Yes, the sea temperature averages 20–22°C in October, which is comfortable for swimming. Many charter guests still enjoy a dip, especially during midday hours when the air temperature reaches 26°C. If you're sensitive to cooler water, consider a wetsuit or a quick splash.

faq.q: Can you still sunbathe on a yacht in October?
faq.a: Absolutely. With average highs of 26°C and low humidity, October offers excellent sunbathing conditions. The UV index is moderate (5–6), so sunscreen is still advised. The Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge have spacious sundecks perfect for lounging without overheating.

faq.q: What is the wind like for sailing in October?
faq.a: October typically sees light to moderate winds (10–15 knots) from the east or west, ideal for smooth cruising. The Poniente (west) wind brings clearer skies, while the Levante (east) can pick up slightly but rarely exceeds 20 knots. Our skippers adjust routes accordingly.

faq.q: Are boat prices cheaper in October?
faq.a: Yes, October is shoulder season, so demand is lower than summer. Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours, and longer charters (8h) at €2,299. Mangusta 80 charters from €4,719/4h include a jet ski. Other boats are priced on request — typically 10–20% below peak rates.

faq.q: Which boat is best for an October charter?
faq.a: For small groups, the Azimut 39 (11 pax) offers a good balance of comfort and value. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) has a flybridge and jet ski included. If you prefer a catamaran, the Lagoon 380 (10 pax) provides stable cruising and plenty of shade.

faq.q: What should I wear for a yacht charter in October?
faq.a: Bring layers: a light jumper or jacket for the morning/evening (18°C), and swimwear/shorts for midday. A windbreaker is useful if you're on the flybridge. Non-slip shoes are recommended. Don't forget sunglasses and sunscreen — the sun is still strong.

faq.q: Is October a good time to see dolphins in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, October is a great month for dolphin sightings, especially off the coast of Cabopino and Cala del Faro. Common dolphins and bottlenose dolphins are frequently spotted. Early morning charters (departing 09:00) increase your chances. The Mangusta 80's speed helps cover more ground.

faq.q: Can I charter a boat for a sunset cruise in October?
faq.a: Definitely. Sunset in October is around 19:30, so a 2-hour charter from 18:00 offers golden light and a relaxed atmosphere. The Azimut 58 Flybridge or Astondoa 40 are popular choices. Sunset cruises are often cheaper than daytime charters — ask for a quote via WhatsApp.

data.body_html:
October in Marbella delivers a sweet spot: warm enough for swimming, calm seas, and significantly fewer crowds than July or August. If you're wondering whether a Marbella yacht charter weather October works for a late-season holiday, the short answer is yes — with average air temperatures of 22°C and sea temperatures of 20–22°C, it's one of the best months for a relaxed Mediterranean escape. Here's what you need to know before you book.

October Weather in Marbella: What to Expect

Marbella enjoys a microclimate that keeps October pleasantly warm. Daytime highs reach 26°C, with lows around 18°C in the evening. The sun shines for about 11 hours per day (sunrise 07:30, sunset 19:30), giving you ample time on the water. Rainfall is minimal — typically 4–6 days across the month, totalling 40–60 mm. The sea remains swimmable at 20–22°C, so you can still take a dip between anchor stops. Winds are generally light (10–15 knots), making for smooth cruising along the Costa del Sol.

Why October Is a Smart Time for a Yacht Charter

Demand drops after September, which means more availability and often lower prices. Our Boat Rental Marbella fleet is fully operational from Puerto Banús, with all boats available on request. You'll find quieter anchorages at Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and Cabopino — no jostling for a mooring buoy. The water clarity is excellent, and the light for photography is stunning. Plus, the Golden Mile and Marbella Marina are less crowded, so your pre- or post-charter lunch feels more exclusive.

Best Boats for an October Charter

For small groups (up to 5–6 pax), the Speedboat (7m) or Dubhe (8m) offer a nimble, budget-friendly option. For up to 11 guests, the Azimut 39 (€749/2h) is a favourite — its spacious cockpit and sunpad are ideal for October's milder sun. If you're celebrating a special occasion, the Mangusta 80 (white or grey, 24m, 12 pax, from €4,719/4h) includes a jet ski and a flybridge for panoramic views. The Ferretti 94 (29m, 12 pax) and Canados 86 (26m, 12 pax) are superb for luxury charters with full crew. For a stable, family-friendly ride, the Lagoon 380 catamaran (11.6m, 10 pax) is hard to beat.

What's Included in Every Charter

All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Onboard you'll find complimentary water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There are no hidden fees — the price you see is the price you pay. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39), the 2-hour rate is €749 and the 8-hour rate is €2,299. Mangusta 80 variants start at €4,719 for 4 hours. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Where to Cruise in October

With calm seas and pleasant temperatures, October is perfect for exploring the coast. Popular routes include west to Estepona and Sotogrande (about 15 NM from Puerto Banús), or east to Cabopino and Cala del Faro (10 NM). The Río Verde area offers a scenic stop near the Marbella Club. For a longer day, head to Cala Cortés — a quiet cove with crystal-clear water. Your skipper will tailor the itinerary to your preferences.

October Events and Dining Ideas

October in Marbella is quieter but still lively. The Marbella International Film Festival often takes place this month, and many beach clubs (like Ocean Club and Nikki Beach) remain open until late October. For a post-charter meal, try El Pescador in Puerto Banús for fresh seafood, or Skina (Michelin-starred) in the old town. If you're anchoring near Cabopino, Chiringuito El Caballo serves excellent grilled fish right on the sand.

How to Book Your October Charter

Booking is straightforward. Browse our fleet, pick your preferred boat, and send us a WhatsApp message with your date, group size, and desired duration. We'll confirm availability and send a quote. For Tier A boats, you can book directly online. For others, we'll provide pricing within hours. October slots fill quickly for weekends, so early booking is recommended. All boats depart from Puerto Banús, with free parking nearby.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is it warm enough to swim in Marbella in October?

Yes, the sea temperature averages 20–22°C in October, which is comfortable for swimming. Many charter guests still enjoy a dip, especially during midday hours when the air temperature reaches 26°C. If you're sensitive to cooler water, consider a wetsuit or a quick splash.

Can you still sunbathe on a yacht in October?

Absolutely. With average highs of 26°C and low humidity, October offers excellent sunbathing conditions. The UV index is moderate (5–6), so sunscreen is still advised. The Mangusta 80 and Azimut 58 Flybridge have spacious sundecks perfect for lounging without overheating.

What is the wind like for sailing in October?

October typically sees light to moderate winds (10–15 knots) from the east or west, ideal for smooth cruising. The Poniente (west) wind brings clearer skies, while the Levante (east) can pick up slightly but rarely exceeds 20 knots. Our skippers adjust routes accordingly.

Are boat prices cheaper in October?

Yes, October is shoulder season, so demand is lower than summer. Tier A boats like the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 start at €749 for 2 hours, and longer charters (8h) at €2,299. Mangusta 80 charters from €4,719/4h include a jet ski. Other boats are priced on request — typically 10–20% below peak rates.

Which boat is best for an October charter?

For small groups, the Azimut 39 (11 pax) offers a good balance of comfort and value. For larger parties, the Mangusta 80 (12 pax) has a flybridge and jet ski included. If you prefer a catamaran, the Lagoon 380 (10 pax) provides stable cruising and plenty of shade.

What should I wear for a yacht charter in October?

Bring layers: a light jumper or jacket for the morning/evening (18°C), and swimwear/shorts for midday. A windbreaker is useful if you're on the flybridge. Non-slip shoes are recommended. Don't forget sunglasses and sunscreen — the sun is still strong.

Is October a good time to see dolphins in Marbella?

Yes, October is a great month for dolphin sightings, especially off the coast of Cabopino and Cala del Faro. Common dolphins and bottlenose dolphins are frequently spotted. Early morning charters (departing 09:00) increase your chances. The Mangusta 80's speed helps cover more ground.

Can I charter a boat for a sunset cruise in October?

Definitely. Sunset in October is around 19:30, so a 2-hour charter from 18:00 offers golden light and a relaxed atmosphere. The Azimut 58 Flybridge or Astondoa 40 are popular choices. Sunset cruises are often cheaper than daytime charters — ask for a quote via WhatsApp.

October is a hidden gem for a Marbella yacht charter. With reliable weather, lower prices, and uncrowded coves, it's the perfect time to experience the Costa del Sol from the water. Check our fleet and message us to secure your dates.
